title: Smoky Peppery Japanese Salmon Soup
description: A light, peppery soup that blends smoky salmon with miso broth, fresh ginger, and tofu for a lazy Sunday meal.
instructions: 1. In a saucepan, heat the vegetable broth over medium heat until it begins to simmer.
2. Add the soy sauce, mirin, miso paste, minced ginger, minced garlic, sliced shiitake mushrooms, and sesame oil; whisk to dissolve the miso.
3. Reduce the heat to low and let the broth infuse for 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
4. Increase the heat back to a gentle simmer and stir in the sliced smoked salmon, tofu cubes, and chili flakes if using.
5. Simmer the soup for an additional 3 minutes, just until the tofu warms through and the salmon is heated; avoid boiling to preserve texture.
6. Taste and adjust seasoning with more soy sauce or black pepper as desired.
7. Ladle the soup into bowls, then sprinkle the reserved sliced green onions on top for a fresh crunch.
8. Serve immediately with a side of steamed rice or a crisp cucumber salad.
9. For a vegetarian version, substitute smoked salmon with smoked tofu and add sliced napa cabbage for extra bulk.
10. Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator; reheat gently to avoid over‑cooking the salmon.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
